course_description:
This course provides students with techniques for evaluating investments on an individual basis and in the context of portfolio theory and asset pricing. Techniques for analysing investments focus on maximising expected returns while minimising risk are covered.  Fixed income security analysis and fund performance are detailed.

This course covers the two main types of investmentsﾠ- equity and debt investments - along with a week on alternative investments and a week on ethics and standards . Our objective is to develop skills in pricing financial assets and forming portfolios which maximise expected return for a given level of risk.

The way this course is integrated with other finance courses is as follows. Note that these are not necessarily the only topics covered in the other courses. The table below illustrates this integration.

FINM2415 Corporate Finance considers the financial decisions managers undertake in order to minimise their weighted average cost of capital, the components of which are the cost of debt and equity capital. We also need to estimate the cost of debt and equity capital in order to value investments and construct portfolios.

FINM3403 International Financial Management considers the international exposure of investments by discussing exchange rate risk, foreign currency derivatives, political risk and international taxation.

FINM3404 Banking and Lending Decisions considers credit risk in various lending situations and the information content of financial statements in assessing this risk.

FINM3405 Derivatives and Risk Management discusses the use of derivatives as a risk management technique. Derivatives allow portfolio managers to structure portfolios to achieve a specified level of risk and expected return.

FINM2416 topics

Relationship to other courses

Securities markets

FINM2415 Corporate Finance: Raising equity capital in initial public offers and secondary equity offers

Equity investment

FINM2415 Corporate Finance: Valuation of initial public offers and acquisitions using discounted cash flow techniques

Debt investments

FINM3404 Banking and Lending Decisions: Credit risk measured using financial ratios

Portfolio management

FINM2415 Corporate Finance: Cost of equity capital estimation using the Capital Asset Pricing Model

FINM3403 International Financial Management: Extension to maximising the reward-for-risk trade-off in an international equities portfolio

FINM3405 Derivatives and Risk Management: Extension to portfolio management to incorporate derivatives to manage risk exposure.

Course Changes in Response to Previous Student Feedback

In response to student feedback, we are introducing a portfolio simulation component into the assessment.

course_aims: This course provides students with techniques for pricing financial investments on an individual basis and evaluating in the context of a portfolio. Techniques for analysing portfolio investments focus on maximising expected returns while minimising risk. Therefore, students should achieve the ability to value investments and quantify the contribution they make to the risk of a portfolio. Students will also learn the role of ethics and professional standards within the investment management process.

learning_outcomes.description: Understand factors that impact financial markets.
Analyse financial data in order to assess a particular investment or investment strategy.
Perform and evaluate different techniques to value equity, debt, and alternative investments.
Explain and evaluate the risks associated with investing, including understanding ethical and professional standards in an investment management context.
Analyse techniques for portfolio creation and to measure portfolio performance.

assessment_details.title: Equity Valuation and Portfolio Simulation
assessment_details.category: Paper/ Report/ Annotation, Role play/ Simulation
assessment_details.due_date: Individual equity valuation report submission 23/03/2026 - 2/04/2026Group initial portfolio holding period 2/04/2026 - 23/04/2026Group revision of portfolio 24/04/2026 - 24/04/2026New revised portfolio holding period 24/04/2026 - 8/05/2026Group portfolio simulation report submission 9/05/2026 - 14/05/2026
assessment_details.other_conditions: Peer assessed, Work integrated learning, Longitudinal.See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This assessment consists of two integrated components.As a group of 5–6 students, members will jointly select a number of equities equal to the group size and use these stocks toconstruct and actively manage a AUD 10,000 simulated investment portfolio, evaluating its performance and risk relative to a benchmark. For benchmarking purposes, use the ISHARES CORE S&P/ASX 200 ETF (replicating ASX 200) as the benchmark portfolio.Individually, each student will conduct adiscounted cash flow (DCF) valuation of one company that is included in the group’s portfolioand provide a justified investment recommendation.This assessment evaluates students’collaborative portfolio management skillsas well as theirindividual ability to apply valuation techniquesand assess investment decisions in a real-world investment context.In writing your Investment Policy Statement (IPS),Your individual equity valuation reportis no more than 2 pages and typed in 12pt Times New Roman with 1.5 line spacing and normal margin. (Submission deadline: 02/04/2026)Your group portfolio simulation reportis no more than 2,500 words with 10% allowance and typed in 12pt Times New Roman with 1.5 line spacing and normal margin. (Submission deadline: 14/05/2026)AI Statement:This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance.A failure to reference generative AI use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI tools.Read full task descriptionHide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
The assignment must be submitted to both Turnitin AND Blackboard submission links. For more details, refers to the "Assessment Guidelines" on Blackboard.
Deferral or extension
You may be able toapply for an extension.
The maximum extension allowed is 5 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
This assessment includes both individual and group components, and the portfolio simulations are scheduled for specific dates and weeks. Therefore, while extensions may be granted for individual submissions, extensions are not available for group submissions.
Late submission
Apenaltyof 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able toapply for an extension.
assessment_details.late_submission: Apenaltyof 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.

learning_activities.topic: Behavioral FinanceEfficient Market Hypothesis, Behavioral Biases, and Technical AnalysisLearning outcomes:L01, L02
Tutorial 1: Behavioral Finance & Group formation for Equity Valuation and Portfolio SimulationLearning outcomes:L01, L02
Financial Statement AnalysisFinancial statement and ratio analysisLearning outcomes:L02
Tutorial 2: Financial Statement Analysis & Group finalization for Equity Valuation and Portfolio SimulationLearning outcomes:L02
Stock ValuationReview and extension on dividend discount models, growth assumptions, and present value of growth opportunities (PVGO)Learning outcomes:L03
Tutorial 3: Stock ValuationLearning outcomes:L03
DCF Valuation and Portfolio Simulation BasicsFree cash flow valuation and Introduction of Portfolio simulationLearning outcomes:L03
Tutorial 4: DCF Valuation and Introduction of Portfolio Simulation PlatformLearning outcomes:L03

Macro and Industry AnalysisOther forms of fundamental analysis, including the global economy, the domestic macroeconomy, demand and supply shocks, federal government policy, business cycles, and industry analysis.Learning outcomes:L01, L02

Tutorial 5: Macro and Industry analysis & Initial Portfolio ConstructionGood Friday Public Holiday - Friday 3rd April 2026- Check Blackboard for announcements about affected classes.Learning outcomes:L01, L02
Portfolio Theory 1Risk, return, and the historical record; capital allocation to risky assets; and optimal risky portfoliosLearning outcomes:L02, L04, L05
In-Semester Break
Tutorial 6: Portfolio Theory 1Learning outcomes:L02, L04, L05

Portfolio Theory 2Various types of pricing models, including the Capital Asset Pricing Model (CAPM), Arbirage Pricing Theory (APT), and multifactor models of risks and returns.Learning outcomes:L04, L05
Tutorial 7: Portfolio Theory 2 & Portfolio RevisionLearning outcomes:L04, L05
Portfolio Theory 3Portfolio performance evaluation, including the conventional theory of performance evaluation, style analysis, and performance attribution procedures.Learning outcomes:L03, L04, L05
Tutorial 8: Portfolio Theory 3Learning outcomes:L03, L04, L05
Fixed Income SecuritiesBond prices and term structure, ESG considerations in fixed incomeLearning outcomes:L03

Tutorial 9: Fixed Income Securities & Finalization of Portfolio SimulationLabour Day Public Holiday - Monday 4th May 2026- Check Blackboard for announcements about affected classes.Learning outcomes:L03
Bond PortfoliosInterest rate risk - duration and convexity, credit risk, asset and liability management, ESG investingLearning outcomes:L03
Tutorial 10: Bond PortfoliosLearning outcomes:L03
Alternative InvestmentsIntroducing derivative securities and their pricingLearning outcomes:L02, L03, L04
Tutorial 11: Alternative InvestmentsLearning outcomes:L02, L03, L04
Ethics and Professional Standards in the Investment IndustryTrading strategies involving options and pricing forwards and futuresLearning outcomes:L02, L03, L04
Tutorial 12: Ethics and Professional Standards in the Investment Industry & Final Exam PracticeLearning outcomes:L02, L03, L04

Review MaterialsCode of ethics, standards of professional conduct, ethical framework, guidance and application of the code and standards, presentation of performance results for the Global Investment Performance Standards and review of all materials.Learning outcomes:L01, L02, L03, L04, L05
